sourceRepository.preservationRole: source history, citation context, and audit provenance

artifactFamilies.description: Multi-case stratified corpus covering American Revolution, Napoleon, Lincoln, and Hitler under Koenigsberg's Law of Sacrifice; manifest-driven pipeline with 41-document baseline
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Texts before ~1928 are generally public domain in the US. Nazi-era texts: some are out of copyright; translations and scholarly apparatus require review. American Revolution has a PPW case folder and reference-only subset link; Napoleon remains proposed and must have a case folder before import.

artifactFamilies.description: Cross-case synthesis methodology, reliability sampling design, and corpus expansion tracking documentation
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Original work of this project; no third-party rights apply to methodology documents.

artifactFamilies.description: Quarto research site for cross-case synthesis, including per-case corpus pages and comparative analysis outputs
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Child-repo site remains live as primary publication. PPW site will reference it.

artifactFamilies.description: Manifest-driven pipeline scripts for corpus ingestion, stratification, and validation
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Child-repo-specific; inform PPW pipeline design but are not imported.

supersessionNote: Child-repo issue tracking superseded by PPW Milestone 12 migration. New implementation work is coordinated through PPW. Source repository is preserved as history and audit context.
